summary: Removed an empty assembly source file, asm/melee/ft/ftlib.s, and removed its corresponding object entry from obj_files.mk. The PR was a small build-list cleanup after ftlib had a C object entry present, avoiding retention of a blank asm file in the build inputs.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/ftlib.s, which contained only a single blank line.
Left the nearby C object entry $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftlib.c.o intact, indicating the assembly stub was no longer needed as a separate build input.
smart_moves: Kept the cleanup minimal: deleted only the empty asm file and its obj_files.mk reference.
Updated the build object list together with the file deletion, preventing the build system from looking for a removed assembly object.
Removed dead assembly scaffolding rather than preserving a placeholder with no contents.
decomp_lessons: When an asm file becomes empty or obsolete, remove both the file and its corresponding obj_files.mk entry in the same change.
Do not leave empty .s files as placeholders if the matching or migrated C source is already represented in TEXT_O_FILES.
Small repository hygiene changes can reduce confusion in later decomp work by making the object list reflect actual source inputs.
naming_conventions: The ftlib module appears as src/melee/ft/ftlib.c.o in obj_files.mk after this cleanup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR did not introduce a matching tactic; it removed an empty asm artifact.
The practical matching/build tactic is to keep TEXT_O_FILES synchronized with the current source-of-truth files and remove blank asm entries once they are not contributing code.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that remove obsolete asm files after C migration
Find changes updating obj_files.mk TEXT_O_FILES for ftlib
Find empty .s file cleanup PRs in asm/melee/ft
